questions.question: Which of the following is a matrix representation of a graph where the entry at row i and column j is 1 if there is an edge between vertex i and vertex j, and 0 otherwise?
questions.explanation: An Adjacency Matrix is a matrix where the entry at row i and column j is 1 if there is an edge between vertex i and vertex j, and 0 otherwise.

questions.question: What is the main difference between an Adjacency Matrix and an Incidence Matrix?
options: A) Incidence Matrix is used for planar graphs
B) Adjacency Matrix is used for weighted graphs
C) Adjacency Matrix shows all edges, Incidence Matrix shows only incident edges
D) Incidence Matrix is used for directed graphs
questions.explanation: An Adjacency Matrix shows all edges, while an Incidence Matrix shows only the incident edges between a vertex and the rest of the graph.

questions.question: Which matrix representation of a graph is used to show the edges of the graph that form a cycle?
questions.explanation: A Circuit Matrix is a matrix representation of a graph where the entry at row i and column j is 1 if there is an edge between vertex i and vertex j, and the edge forms a cycle.

questions.question: What is the main use of a Path Matrix?
options: A) To show the edges of the graph that form a cycle
B) To show the edges that belong to a specific path
C) To show the incident edges between a vertex and the rest of the graph
D) To show the connected components of the graph
questions.explanation: A Path Matrix is used to show the edges that belong to a specific path between two vertices.

questions.question: Which matrix representation of a graph is used to show both the edges and the vertices of the graph?
questions.explanation: An Incidence Matrix is a matrix representation of a graph where the entry at row i and column j represents whether vertex i is incident to edge j or not.
